agent_notes: Quality over volume: Wandsworth produced a strong PAINTS set for wine bars, markets, and two approved walks, but only sparse qualifying museum/gallery and books-and-coffee options.
Creative Walks are limited to Battersea Park and Wandsworth Common because Tooting Bec Common and Putney Heath are explicitly removed in the PAINTS v6 safety list.
Opening hours, walk-in policy, and group tolerance are source-backed where available; any venue-level operational point not found live is marked UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.

venues.opening_hours: Official site confirms address; Harden's lists Mon 5pm, Tue-Sat from 12pm, Sun closed. Host must confirm exact event-date hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-ins welcomed per SquareMeal FAQ; peak dinner booking advised.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Small 34-35 cover room; group of 8-12 likely needs heads-up call or early bar seating.
venues.capacity_notes: Press/review sources describe a compact neighbourhood wine-bar restaurant with booths and counter/bar area.
venues.best_time_slot: Tue-Thu 5:30-7:30pm or Saturday lunch; avoid prime Friday/Saturday dinner.
venues.why_fits_paints: A small neighbourhood restaurant with a serious low-intervention wine list, tactile room, and a real before-and-after story. It feels conversational rather than performative and gives attendees a good owner/chef anecdote immediately. The food is more expensive than a casual bar, but the wine-bar identity is strong enough for a PAINTS Show & Tell.
venues.anecdote_story: Opened in the former Other Side Fried Battersea site by Matt Harris and Tommy Kempton; reviewers repeatedly note the transformation from chicken shop to polished wine-led bistro.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Low-intervention wines, modern French/British small plates, seasonal sharing dishes.
venues.safety_notes: Busy Battersea Rise/Clapham Junction corridor with multiple transport routes; late-evening host should direct attendees to Clapham Junction via main roads.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 2-3 min walk via Battersea Rise / St John's Road
venues.risks_watchouts: Small room and higher spend; walk-ins may be hard at peak. Host should call before publishing.

venues.walk_in_policy: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm; site offers booking but does not clearly state walk-ins for 8-12.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely possible early evening with advance call; demoted because group walk-in acceptance is not explicit.
venues.capacity_notes: Natural wine bar and food venue; no public capacity found.
venues.why_fits_paints: A true independent from Sunny Hodge, explicitly built around natural, organic, and low-intervention wine. The Battersea Square edge gives it a tucked-away feel while still being reachable. It is PAINTS-coded, but operational uncertainty keeps it Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: Sister bar to Diogenes the Dog and run by wine science figure Sunny Hodge, which gives the host an easy story about demystifying natural wine.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Natural and organic wines, low-intervention bottles, seasonal food.
venues.safety_notes: Quiet residential Battersea; keep arrival/leaving on Westbridge Road/Battersea Square and avoid isolated river detours after dark.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying drinks backup found within 10 min - closest qualifying alternative is Blackbook Winery at about 14-16 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Backup gap and unverified walk-in/group policy are the binding constraints.

venues.opening_hours: Official bar page says summer wine bar open from 1 May 2026, Friday 4-9pm and Saturday 5:30-9:30pm.
venues.walk_in_policy: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm group walk-in; public page invites visitors to come down during bar hours.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Atmospheric barrel-room setting, but limited Fri/Sat windows and group acceptance require confirmation.
venues.capacity_notes: Urban winery among barrels; no public capacity found.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is one of the few London wine experiences where the making story is physically in the room. The industrial-estate setting rewards curiosity and gives the event a strong 'show' element. It is Tier B only because hours are limited and the backup radius is weak.
venues.anecdote_story: Blackbook is a working Battersea winery, and the summer bar lets guests drink among barrels behind Queenstown Road.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Sustainable London-made wine, tastings, barrel-room summer wine bar.
venues.safety_notes: Industrial estate setting; host should use daytime/early evening only and direct group to main roads and Queenstown Road/Battersea Park stations.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying drinks backup found within 10 min - closest qualifying alternative is Aspen & Meursault at about 14-16 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Only two weekly service windows; weather/industrial-estate feel may not suit late evening.

venues.opening_hours: Official/market pages list Tue 12-5, Wed-Sat 12-22:30, Sun 12-17, Mon closed.
venues.walk_in_policy: Market bar model; walk-ins appear normal, but group of 8-12 must be confirmed with venue.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a loose market-table setup; best early evening before Tooting Market peaks.
venues.capacity_notes: Unit 16A in Tooting Market with shop, bar, and tasting-room identity.
venues.why_fits_paints: A small independent natural-wine bar inside an indoor market gives the right mixture of low-stakes sociability and texture. It also balances Battersea-heavy sourcing with a strong Tooting pick. The female/small-maker wine emphasis is a good PAINTS conversation hook.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue evolved from the long-running Unwined presence into Indie Wine Club, championing low-intervention bottles from small and often female winemakers.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Low-intervention wine by glass/bottle, tastings, independent winemaker focus.
venues.safety_notes: Use Tooting Broadway and main high-street exits; avoid late dispersal through quieter back streets.
venues.risks_watchouts: Market can become loud and crowded on Friday/Saturday evenings; group seating is not guaranteed.

venues.walk_in_policy: Official FAQ says walk-ins are welcome, bookings encouraged Thu-Sun.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Operationally strong for a group; less off-grid than ideal.
venues.capacity_notes: Wine bar/restaurant with group dining and wine tasting options.
venues.why_fits_paints: Reliable, wine-led, and close to stronger PAINTS backups, but more polished and multi-site than the ideal. It is useful as an operational backup to Ploussard. Organic/independent vineyard messaging keeps it above the floor.
venues.anecdote_story: Humble Grape's hook is sourcing from organic and sustainable independent vineyards and letting guests drink bottles in at retail-led pricing offers.
venues.safety_notes: Central Battersea Rise; straightforward return to Clapham Junction via main roads.
venues.risks_watchouts: Multi-site feel; avoid overusing if the PAINTS route needs a more independent texture.

venues.opening_hours: Official site says cafe daily 7:30-16:00 and wine bar Wed-Sat from 18:00 until late; Instagram says Wed-Sat 18:00-23:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm for 8-12; cafe/wine-bar model appears walk-in friendly.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely best for a casual early cheese-and-wine session, but group seating needs confirmation.
venues.capacity_notes: Independent community cafe that converts into a wine bar; no public capacity found.
venues.why_fits_paints: A community cafe becoming an evening wine bar has the right neighbourhood transformation story. It is softer and newer than Ploussard, making it better as a low-pressure Tier B. The Fourways coffee connection gives a daytime backup angle too.
venues.anecdote_story: Opened as an independent cafe on St John's Road and added cheese-and-wine evenings after dark.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Coffee by day; wine, cheese, and casual evening drinks Wed-Sat.
venues.safety_notes: Main Battersea/Clapham Junction high street with good footfall; use Clapham Junction route.
venues.risks_watchouts: New venue; ratings/review depth may be below PAINTS gate until host checks Google live.

venues.opening_hours: Instagram and local press list Wed-Sat from 17:00; host must confirm exact closing time/event-date service.
venues.walk_in_policy: Instagram states walk-ins only unless 6 or more; group of 8-12 must book/contact.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Demoted because groups over 6 need arrangement and the space is deliberately hidden/intimate.
venues.capacity_notes: Pop-up/hidden wine bar behind Ground Coffee Society; no public capacity found.
venues.why_fits_paints: A hidden after-hours wine bar in Putney is exactly the kind of slightly-off-grid discovery PAINTS wants. The pop-up model creates a good story about idle spaces being repurposed. Operationally, the group policy needs careful confirmation.
venues.anecdote_story: The Wine Escape turns underused neighbourhood spaces into evening wine bars; the Putney site operates through 'The Back Door' behind a cafe.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine by glass/bottle in a small secret-bar setting.
venues.safety_notes: Lower Richmond Road is a main route; keep dispersal toward Putney Bridge/Putney stations and avoid riverside shortcuts late.
venues.risks_watchouts: Pop-up model and group-over-6 rule make this a host-confirm pick.

venues.opening_hours: OpenTable lists daily 17:00-23:00; host must confirm against venue website/socials.
venues.walk_in_policy: OpenTable says not on booking network and to contact directly; group walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possibly suitable early evening, but insufficient public evidence for Tier A.
venues.capacity_notes: Small independent wine bar serving wine, cheese, cold meats and craft beer.
venues.why_fits_paints: Simple, independent, and geographically useful for Putney. It lacks the stronger story/press texture of the Battersea options, but clears the floor as a low-key backup. Best used when the event needs a west-of-borough counterweight.
venues.anecdote_story: No deep backstory found beyond independent neighbourhood wine-bar positioning.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine, cheese, cold meats, selected craft beer.
venues.safety_notes: Upper Richmond Road has good footfall and transport links; avoid late-night riverside route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Public review depth and walk-in policy need host verification.

venues.opening_hours: Ram Quarter official page lists Saturday 10:30-15:00; Wandsworth Town events lists upcoming Saturday markets in July 2026.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; no walk-in gate needed for market. Meeting-point walk-in must be host confirmed.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good open-air/market movement for 8-12; meet outside Story Coffee/Ram Quarter entrance.
venues.capacity_notes: Weekly market with street food and produce traders; open public environment.
venues.why_fits_paints: The old brewery/Ram Quarter setting gives a market day more story than a generic row of stalls. It has food, producer atmosphere, and a good central-Wandsworth meeting point. Saturday daytime is operationally clean.
venues.anecdote_story: The market sits in the Ram Quarter, Wandsworth's former brewery site, giving the host a neighbourhood-regeneration and industrial-history frame.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Ethically sourced produce, street food, rotating local traders.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Wandsworth High Street/Ram Quarter has good footfall; avoid river-edge shortcuts after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15 min by short bus/taxi or 30+ min walk; market backup within relaxed 15 min transit rule
venues.risks_watchouts: Trader mix can vary; host should check weekly roster and weather.

venues.opening_hours: London Farmers Markets lists every Saturday 09:00-13:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access farmers market; meeting-point walk-in must be host confirmed.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for a slow Saturday food-and-producer circuit; meet at Backstory if open and uncongested.
venues.capacity_notes: School-site farmers market with variable weekly traders.
venues.why_fits_paints: Producer-led, low-pressure, and close to Backstory for a proper PAINTS coffee/bookshop anchor. It is not glamorous, but it has the right human-scale rhythm for strangers to talk. Balham also helps balance the Battersea cluster.
venues.anecdote_story: Established in September 2011 according to London Farmers Markets.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fresh produce, pastries, coffee, bread, meat, eggs and rotating farmers-market stalls.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime school/neighbourhood setting near Balham station; keep meetup on main roads.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10-12 min by Northern line/bus; market backup within relaxed 15 min transit rule
venues.risks_watchouts: Not all stalls attend weekly; check trader list/weather.

venues.opening_hours: Official trader pages show individual hours; market social says check individual traders. Host must confirm selected stalls.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access indoor market; individual trader seating is walk-in but not guaranteed for 8-12.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a roaming format rather than one fixed table; use Indie Wine Club as a timed anchor.
venues.capacity_notes: Indoor market with food, drink, retail, fabric, jewellery, and independent traders.
venues.why_fits_paints: Tooting Market is busy, plural, and full of sensory prompts for conversation. It has a stronger independent-trader identity than many London indoor markets. The best PAINTS version pairs browsing with a wine or food stop rather than trying to sit everyone in one place for two hours.
venues.anecdote_story: The market has a long multicultural history and 2022 plaques celebrating Tooting as 'a community of all, for all' are noted in local history sources.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Independent food/drink stalls, fabric/homeware, jewellery, tailoring, market retail.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall daytime/evening market by Tooting Broadway; host should keep group inside main market and high-street exits.
venues.risks_watchouts: Friday nights can be loud/crowded; some traders keep different hours.

venues.opening_hours: Official site describes 100+ stalls under one roof; older local source lists Mon 09:30-18:30, Tue-Sat 09:30-22:00. Host must verify 2026 hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access indoor market; trader seating walk-in varies.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Works as a roaming market, not a fixed-table venue.
venues.capacity_notes: Large indoor market with more than 100 stalls.
venues.why_fits_paints: Broadway Market is less polished but has the right covered-market resilience and local texture. It is best as a backup or paired second act to Tooting Market. The under-one-roof format helps if the weather turns.
venues.anecdote_story: Official site describes it as established in 1936 and one of South London's oldest markets.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Food, drink, groceries, clothes, electronics, homewares and varied market stalls.
venues.safety_notes: Use Tooting Broadway high-street entrances and daytime/early evening hours.
venues.risks_watchouts: Some TripAdvisor sentiment notes early arrivals may find few stalls open; avoid first hour.

venues.opening_hours: Official/local listings show Mon-Sat 10:00-17:30, Sun 12:00-17:00, open most bank holidays 12:00-17:00.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good browsing format for 8-12, with nearby coffee/wine anchors for start/end.
venues.why_fits_paints: It has immediate show-and-tell potential: objects, eras, and oddities give strangers something to talk about without forcing intimacy. It is more tactile and visually varied than a standard food market. The slight obviousness penalty is offset by the quality of browsing.
venues.anecdote_story: Long-running Battersea antiques market with over 30 dealers selling vintage, retro, art deco, mirrors, prints and collectables.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Antiques, vintage objects, retro design, collectables, pictures, mirrors, furniture.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Northcote Road has high footfall; use Clapham Junction route.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15 min by short bus/taxi; market backup within relaxed 15 min transit rule
venues.risks_watchouts: Not food-led; pair with independent coffee/drinks for pacing.

venues.venue_name: Battersea Park Art, Pagoda and Pump House Loop
venues.opening_hours: Wandsworth Council lists opening times 8am until dusk; park gates 6:30am-10:30pm. Use daytime only for PAINTS.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park; meeting-point cafe walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for 8-12 as a daylight walk with 3-4 stops and a weather backup.
venues.capacity_notes: 200-acre Victorian park with broad paths, riverside promenade, lake and notable landmarks.
venues.why_fits_paints: Battersea Park is on the PAINTS Premium Launch Parks list, so it is the safest Creative Walk in the borough. The route has art, landscape, industrial history and river light without needing to leave the park. It supports a calm 2-hour format for strangers.
venues.anecdote_story: Suggested stops: Peace Pagoda; Pump House Gallery/former 1861 pumping house; lake/cascades; Festival Gardens/Brown Dog story. The park itself was built from former Battersea Fields and opened as a Victorian public park.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Creative walk with park design, Peace Pagoda, Pump House exterior, lake, river promenade, public monuments.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS Premium Launch Park - confirmed. Daytime only; avoid isolated riverside or wooded stretches after dusk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather and park-event closures; host should check Battersea Park events/fireworks/private-event notices.

venues.venue_name: Wandsworth Common Heritage and Lake Walk
venues.opening_hours: Council page describes common facilities; GoParks lists always open. Use daylight only for PAINTS.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access common; meeting-point cafe walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a gentle daylight route; keep to main paths and lake/common edges.
venues.capacity_notes: Large common with woods, lakes, heath, cafe, sports and trim trail.
venues.why_fits_paints: Wandsworth Common is on the PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist, making it the borough's second legitimate Creative Walk option. The common has enough visible heritage to structure conversation without becoming a history lecture. It also avoids over-clustering around Battersea.
venues.anecdote_story: Suggested stops: Bellevue Road edge; lake; former Battersea West Heath/boundary-marker story; Royal Victoria Patriotic Building exterior context; heritage boards/common-protection story.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Creative walk with lake, heath, woods, heritage boards and common-land history.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist - confirmed. Use daytime only and avoid wooded/quiet stretches near dusk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Less visually dramatic than Battersea Park; weather backup cafe capacity must be confirmed.

venues.venue_name: Royal College of Art Battersea - RCA2026 School of Design EXPO
venues.opening_hours: RCA events page lists School of Design EXPO 16-19 July 2026 at Battersea; exact daily times/entry must be confirmed.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free public exhibition series per RCA; timed-entry/registration availability UNVERIFIED - host must confirm before publishing.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong if public entry remains available; graduate shows suit roaming and discussion.
venues.capacity_notes: Campus-wide public exhibition; group entry depends on registration/visitor management.
venues.best_time_slot: Thursday 16 or Friday 17 July 2026, late morning or early afternoon, subject to RCA times.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the strongest current art/design exhibition in the district, and the exhibition itself justifies the event. It gives attendees new work, prototypes, objects and graduate stories rather than a passive white-cube visit. Battersea's design-campus setting also gives geographical and cultural value.
venues.anecdote_story: RCA2026 is the college's free public showcase of graduating art and design talent across selected dates, with the School of Design EXPO at Battersea.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Design Futures, Design Products, Innovation Design Engineering, Intelligent Mobility and Service Design graduate work.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime campus/Queenstown Road area; host should specify entry point and station route.
venues.backup_venue: Pump House Gallery / Battersea Park cultural backup
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15-20 min walk; no strict qualifying gallery backup within 10 min found, so host should treat as operational caveat
venues.risks_watchouts: Event dates are narrow and not the same as run date; availability/registration must be confirmed.

venues.opening_hours: Current exhibition hours not found on a reliable live official programme page; venue/park pages confirm gallery building and park hours. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm current exhibition access and group entry.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Potentially good when an exhibition is open; not publishable without programme confirmation.
venues.capacity_notes: Grade II listed former pump house/gallery in Battersea Park; venue-hire pages show large event capacity but public exhibition capacity varies.
venues.best_time_slot: Daytime only, tied to confirmed exhibition opening.
venues.why_fits_paints: The building and park setting are very PAINTS, but current exhibition evidence was weak. It stays in Tier B as a venue to monitor, not as a ready-to-publish gallery day. Best paired with the Battersea Park walk if programme status is confirmed.
venues.anecdote_story: The building was originally the 1861 pumping house for the park's lake and cascades, later restored and opened as an art gallery/interpretation centre.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Contemporary art when programme is active; Victorian pump-house architecture and Battersea Park context.
venues.safety_notes: Inside PAINTS Premium Launch Park; daytime access only.
venues.backup_venue: RCA Battersea exhibition programme when open
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15-20 min walk; no strict qualifying gallery backup within 10 min found
venues.risks_watchouts: Current exhibition claim could not be verified from a reliable live programme page; host must confirm before use.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon-Wed 10:00-18:00, Thu-Fri 10:00-21:00, Sat-Sun 10:00-18:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Instagram references walk-ins for events; normal shop/bar appears walk-in friendly. Group of 8-12 must be host confirmed.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Very strong if seated early or as a browse-plus-bar format; call ahead for 8-12.
venues.capacity_notes: Bookshop with long central bar serving coffee, soft drinks, beer, wine and cocktails.
venues.why_fits_paints: Backstory is the borough's clearest Books & Coffee fit: real books, real bar, real community. The ex-journalist founder story and events programme give it depth beyond ambience. It is social without losing the literary core.
venues.anecdote_story: Founded by former Economist journalist Tom Rowley, inspired by American bookshop-bar culture; press notes its long bar was designed for conversation.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Curated fiction/non-fiction, author events, coffee, tea, soft drinks, wine, beer, cocktails and weekend nibbles.
venues.safety_notes: Directly on Balham High Road with tube/rail nearby; use main road routes.
venues.backup_venue: Balham Farmers' Market on Saturdays / Morocco Bound Wandsworth by transit
venues.backup_walk_time: Farmers market about 2-3 min walk on Saturdays; no same-category 10-min backup found on non-market days
venues.risks_watchouts: Popular/events can crowd the space; group policy must be confirmed.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon 10:00-18:00, Tue-Wed 10:00-22:00, Thu-Sat 10:00-23:00, Sun 10:00-19:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop/bar/cafe appears walk-in friendly; group of 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a bookshop-bar hangout if seated early; likely event nights need planning.
venues.capacity_notes: Bookshop, bar, cafe and event space in New Acres/Pilots Walk.
venues.why_fits_paints: A fresh bookshop-bar-cafe in Wandsworth Town gives the district a second strong literary anchor. It has evening hours, drinks, coffee, and a community programming identity. The newer opening means host confirmation matters, but the concept is exactly on brief.
venues.anecdote_story: Morocco Bound expanded its south London bookshop/bar/cafe model into Wandsworth Town's New Acres, with local social posts marking it as a new community bookshop.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Books, coffee, beers/wine, events, poetry/live-music style community programming.
venues.safety_notes: Central Wandsworth Town/Ram Quarter area; keep to Wandsworth High Street and station routes.
venues.backup_venue: Ram Quarter Saturday Market / Story Coffee nearby as operational coffee backup
venues.backup_walk_time: About 2-4 min walk; no strict same-category books-and-coffee backup within 10 min found
venues.risks_watchouts: New venue; ratings/review depth and 8-12 seating policy must be checked live before publishing.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Explicitly listed as Removed in PAINTS v6 safety list; cannot be sourced or used as backup regardless of local appeal.
Explicitly listed as Removed in PAINTS v6 safety list; cannot be sourced or used as backup.
Explicitly listed as Removed in PAINTS v6 safety list; also outside a clean Wandsworth-only route.
The Wandsworth location has closed/moved; current official site lists London at Tower Bridge, not Wandsworth.
Wonderful independent bookshop story, but no verified on-site cafe/coffee partner immediately next door for PAINTS Books & Coffee requirement.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Battersea Bookshop at Battersea Power Station
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Shopping-centre/power-station retail environment conflicts with PAINTS 'no malls/shopping centres' aesthetic filter; no stronger coffee-bookshop evidence found.
Multi-site self-pour wine bar inside Battersea Power Station retail precinct; too templated/mall-adjacent for PAINTS Tier A/B despite operational robustness.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Searcys Champagne Bar at Battersea Power Station
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Polished champagne-bar chain in the power-station retail complex; too corporate/occasion-led and not sufficiently independent PAINTS texture.
Extraordinary craft interior, but outside the target borough framing and requires pre-booked guided tours only, so it fails the universal walk-in model.
